payload.text:
Implementar: Especificación: Social Journal Club (Versión Demo/Prototipo)
1. Visión General del Sistema
El módulo Social Journal Club no es un chat genérico. Es un entorno de Discusión Basada en Evidencia (EBD) diseñado para médicos.
Diferenciador Clave: Sustituye los "Likes" por "Reacciones Científicas" (Sólido, Sesgado, etc.).
Motor de IA: Utiliza un "Agente Moderador" (Abogado del Diablo) para romper el sesgo de confirmación.
Estado: Actualmente configurado en "Modo Demo", con datos simulados en memoria y un usuario hardcodeado ("Dr. Demo").
2. Arquitectura de Datos (Data Model)
Para replicar la lógica, necesitas estas estructuras de datos exactas (TypeScript Interfaces).
2.1. Entidad: Perfil Social (SocialProfile)
Representa a un médico o al bot.
code
TypeScript
interface SocialProfile {
  id: string;          // 'me', 'ai-mod', 'u1', etc.
  name: string;        // Nombre visible (ej. "Dr. Demo")
  specialty: string;   // Ej. "Nephrology"
  institution: string; // Ej. "Hospital Clínico"
  badges?: string[];   // Array de strings: ['Top Critic', 'Expert', 'Moderator']
}
Lógica Visual: Si id === 'ai-mod', el avatar debe renderizarse en rojo con un icono de Robot. Si id === 'me', en azul.
2.2. Entidad: Nota/Comentario (CommunityNote)
Estructura recursiva para hilos de discusión.
code
TypeScript
interface CommunityNote {
  id: string;
  articleId: string;       // ID del paper vinculado
  userId: string;
  userProfile: SocialProfile;
  text: string;            // Contenido del comentario
  timestamp: number;
  reaction?: 'solid' | 'biased' | 'practice_change' | 'educational'; // Voto científico
  anchoredText?: string;   // (Opcional) Texto citado del paper
  isAudio?: boolean;       // (Opcional) Si es nota de voz
  audioDuration?: string;  // Ej. "0:45"
  likes: number;
  isAiModerator?: boolean; // Flag crítico para estilo del Bot
  replies?: CommunityNote[]; // Recursividad: Respuestas anidadas
}
2.3. Entidad: Tema de Debate (DebateTopic)
Usado en el Dashboard principal.
code
TypeScript
interface DebateTopic {
  id: string;
  title: string;           // Ej. "SGLT2i en Trasplante"
  category: string;        // Ej. "CKD"
  participantsCount: number;
  controversyLevel: 'High' | 'Medium' | 'Low'; // Determina color de UI (Rojo/Ámbar/Verde)
  consensusSplit: { 
      pro: number;         // Porcentaje (0-100)
      con: number;         // Porcentaje (0-100)
  }; 
  linkedArticle: {         // Paper base obligatorio
      id: string; 
      title: string; 
  };
  isUserProposed?: boolean; // Si fue creado por el usuario en esta sesión
}
3. Lógica de Negocio (Service Layer)
3.1. Cálculo del Consenso (getConsensusStats)
Input: ID del artículo.
Proceso: Iterar sobre todas las notas asociadas a ese ID.
Algoritmo: Contar la frecuencia de cada reaction.
Output: Objeto con porcentajes { solid: 20, biased: 50, ... }. Esto alimenta la barra multicolor en la UI.
3.2. Agente "Abogado del Diablo" (generateDevilAdvocateComment)
Esta es la función de IA más crítica.
Trigger: Se ejecuta si un artículo tiene poca actividad (< 5 comentarios).
Contenido: Selecciona aleatoriamente de un banco de "Desafíos Metodológicos":
"El intervalo de confianza es muy amplio..."
"Excluyeron pacientes con GFR < 15..."
"Financiado por el fabricante..."
Propósito: Forzar al usuario humano a defender o criticar el estudio.
4. Componente A: El "Live Hub" (SocialFeed.tsx)
Ubicación: Pestaña principal "Social".
4.1. Banner de Estado (Demo Mode)
Visual: Caja amarilla (bg-amber-50) en la parte superior.
Texto: "Modo Prototipo: Usuarios y actividad simulada."
Función: Gestionar expectativas del usuario.
4.2. Carrusel de Debates ("Social Pulse")
Diseño: Scroll horizontal (overflow-x-auto). Tarjetas tipo "Card".
Barra "Tira y Afloja" (Tug-of-War):
Elemento visual crítico en la parte inferior de la tarjeta.
Una línea dividida en dos colores: Verde (Izquierda/Pro) y Ámbar (Derecha/Con).
Los anchos (width %) se vinculan directamente a debate.consensusSplit.
Indicador de Controversia:
Si controversyLevel === 'High', mostrar un punto rojo con animación animate-ping (efecto radar) en la esquina superior derecha.
4.3. Feed de Actividad
Items: Mezcla de eventos: "Dr. X comentó", "Dra. Y está leyendo", "Nuevo Debate".
Interacción: Al hacer clic en CUALQUIER item, se debe disparar la función onOpenArticle(article) para abrir el lector inmersivo. El objetivo es el descubrimiento de contenido.
4.4. Modal de "Crear Debate"
Validación: El botón "Crear" debe estar deshabilitado hasta que el usuario seleccione un Artículo Base. No se permiten debates de texto libre sin evidencia vinculada.
5. Componente B: Panel de Discusión (SocialJournalClub.tsx)
Ubicación: Panel lateral (Drawer) dentro del Lector de Artículos.
5.1. Cabecera y Consenso
Etiqueta: Debe decir "Social Journal Club (Simulado)" en color ámbar.
Termómetro Científico: Barra de progreso multicolor fija arriba (h-2 rounded-full).
Verde: Sólido.
Ámbar: Sesgado.
Azul: Cambio Práctica.
Morado: Educativo.
5.2. Automatización del Bot (The Trigger)
Implementar un useEffect con la siguiente lógica exacta:
Al montar el componente, verificar si notes.length < 5.
Si es cierto, iniciar un setTimeout de 5000ms (5 segundos).
Al finalizar el tiempo, inyectar automáticamente una nota del "NephroBot" (Abogado del Diablo) en el store local.
Activar vibración del dispositivo (navigator.vibrate(50)).
5.3. Herramientas de Escritura Rápida (Smart Chips)
Para facilitar la crítica en móviles, incluir una fila de botones encima del input:
[Criticar N]: Inserta "El tamaño de la muestra (N= ) parece insuficiente..."
[Sesgo]: Inserta "Existe un riesgo potencial de sesgo debido a..."
[Citar P]: Inserta "El valor P es marginal..."
5.4. Modo Podcast (TTS)
Botón: "Escuchar Debate" (icono Play/Stop).
Lógica:
Concatenar los textos de los últimos 5 comentarios.
Enviar a la API de Gemini TTS (gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts).
Reproducir el audio resultante.
6. Detalles de Implementación UI (Estilizado)
Para replicar el "Look & Feel" actual (Tailwind CSS):
Bordes y Sombras: Usar rounded-2xl o rounded-3xl para todo. Bordes sutiles (border-slate-100).
Modo Oscuro:
Fondos: bg-slate-900 o bg-slate-950.
Bordes: border-slate-800.
Textos: text-slate-200 (primario), text-slate-500 (secundario).
Bot del Sistema:
Fondo del mensaje: bg-red-50 (claro) o bg-red-900/10 (oscuro).
Borde: border-red-100 o border-red-500/20.
Avatar: Rojo sólido.
7. Flujo de Usuario a Probar (Demo Path)
Al verificar la réplica, sigue este camino para asegurar que funciona:
Abre la pestaña Social. Verifica el banner amarillo.
Haz clic en el debate "SGLT2i in Transplant".
Se abre el artículo. Abre el panel lateral (icono de personas).
Espera 5 segundos.
Verifica que aparece automáticamente el comentario de "NephroBot" con borde rojo.
Haz clic en el chip "Criticar N".
Selecciona la reacción "Sesgado" (triángulo ámbar) y envía.
Verifica que la Barra de Consenso superior actualiza su segmento ámbar.

**Defining New Components**

I'm currently focused on defining the core types necessary for a "Social Journal Club" feature: `SocialProfile`, `CommunityNote`, and `DebateTopic`. I'm considering their attributes and relationships to create a robust structure for the application's social aspects.


**Implementing Social Features**

Now, I'm diving into the actual implementation. I'm focusing on defining core types first, particularly in `types.ts`. Next, I'll be creating the `socialService.ts` to manage the mock data and logic required. Then, I will address the user interface components: `SocialFeed.tsx` and `SocialJournalClub.tsx`, followed by integrating these components into the app's structure, updating `App.tsx` and `ImmersiveReader.tsx` accordingly. Finally, I will address the consensus calculation and the "Devil's Advocate" bot.


**Designing Data and Components**

I've been creating mock data, specifically `MOCK_PROFILES`, `MOCK_DEBATES`, and `MOCK_NOTES`, to serve as the foundation for the social features. I'm also implementing functions to manipulate this data, like retrieving debates, article notes, and adding notes. I'm also including a Devil's Advocate function, along with a function to get consensus stats. Furthermore, I've started designing the `SocialFeed.tsx` and `SocialJournalClub.tsx` components.


**Defining the CommunityNote Component**

I'm currently working on the `CommunityNote` component. I've defined its props: `articleId`, `isDarkMode`, and `onClose`. The component's state includes the list of `notes`, user input text, and the selected reaction. Logic-wise, I'm setting up `useEffect` hooks to load notes and inject a Bot note if the note count is less than 5. My `handleSend` function will create new notes and update the consensus. The UI will have a header, a message list, and an input area with smart chips, a reaction selector, a text input, a send button and a TTS button. I am implementing a distinct color for Bot notes, and may simplify threaded replies for now.


**Integrating Social Tab**

I'm now integrating the Social Journal Club into the Immersive Reader. I'll add a "Users" icon to the ImmersiveReader header to toggle a right-side drawer containing the `SocialJournalClub` component. This aligns with the "Panel de Discusión" prompt, making the social feature easily accessible. I'll also ensure to apply existing Tailwind styling. As for persistence, I'll use a module-level variable for holding state in `services/socialService.ts`, or possibly React state initialized with mocks.


**Implementing UI Integration**

Currently, I'm integrating the Social Journal Club. I'm adding a button to `ImmersiveReader.tsx` to open a drawer containing the `SocialJournalClub` component. Also, I'm integrating a Social tab into the app via `App.tsx`, and I'm rendering the `SocialFeed` there. I have defined the types in `types.ts`, including the `SocialProfile`, `CommunityNote`, and `DebateTopic` interfaces. These type definitions now exist and are ready for use throughout the application. I have also designed `CommunityNote` and others.


**Refining Component Logic**

Now, I'm streamlining the logic within specific components. I need to get the consensus bar, message list, auto-bot, and input with chips right in `SocialJournalClub.tsx`.  The `SocialFeed.tsx` is being refined to include both the carousel and the activity list. I'll make sure `App.tsx` is properly wired up. Also, I need to add the toggle to `ImmersiveReader.tsx`. Finally, I need to ensure the TTS feature in `SocialJournalClub` uses `gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts` and integrate a TTS audio player (simple integration for now).

Here is the specification and the implementation for the **Social Journal Club** module.

### Specification: Social Journal Club (Demo/Prototype)

**1. Data Model Updates (`types.ts`)**
*   **SocialProfile**: Represents users and the AI moderator.
*   **CommunityNote**: Represents comments/annotations with "Scientific Reactions" (Solid, Biased, etc.) instead of likes. Includes flags for AI moderation.
*   **DebateTopic**: Represents trending discussions for the Social Hub.

**2. Service Layer (`services/socialService.ts`)**
*   **Mock Data**: In-memory storage for profiles, active debates, and comment threads.
*   **Consensus Engine**: Calculates the % split of reactions (Solid vs. Biased) for the UI bars.
*   **Bot Trigger**: A specialized function `generateDevilAdvocateComment` that returns a provocative methodological critique to stimulate discussion.

**3. Components**
*   **`SocialFeed.tsx` (New)**: The main "Social" tab view.
    *   **Features**: "Social Pulse" carousel (horizontal scroll), "Live Activity" feed, and a yellow status banner ("Modo Prototipo").
    *   **Visuals**: Debate cards with "Tug-of-War" bars (Green vs Amber) indicating consensus split. Red "Controversy" dots for high-conflict topics.
*   **`SocialJournalClub.tsx` (New)**: The discussion drawer inside the Article Reader.
    *   **Features**:
        *   **Consensus Thermometer**: A multi-color progress bar at the top showing the scientific sentiment of the thread.
        *   **Auto-Bot Trigger**: If `< 5` comments, waits 5 seconds and injects a "Devil's Advocate" comment from `NephroBot` (Red avatar).
        *   **Smart Chips**: Quick buttons above the input (`[Criticar N]`, `[Sesgo]`, `[Citar P]`) for rapid evidence-based critiquing.
        *   **TTS Podcast**: A button to synthesize the last 5 comments into audio using Gemini.
*   **`ImmersiveReader.tsx` (Update)**:
    *   Add a toggle button (Users Icon) in the header to open the `SocialJournalClub` drawer.
*   **`App.tsx` (Update)**:
    *   Add "Social" tab to the sidebar and mobile dock.
    *   Route the "Social" tab to `SocialFeed`.

**4. Visual Style**
*   **Color Palette**: Consistent with existing app (Slate/Blue).
*   **Bot Distinction**: AI content uses Red/Rose tints to distinguish from human users (Blue/Indigo).
*   **Reactions**:
    *   Green (Shield) = Solid
    *   Amber (Triangle) = Biased
    *   Blue (Zap) = Practice Change
    *   Purple (Book) = Educational

---

### Code Implementation

entries.description: Add Social Journal Club data structures
New service to handle social data logic
New component for Social Feed Tab
New component for the Article Reader side panel
Add toggle for Social Journal Club drawer
Add Social tab routing and layout

payload.text: version de escritorio, revisar el modo de busqueda y como funciona el expandir un articulo que deben expandri individual

**Initiating Problem Analysis**

I'm now diving into the user request, breaking it down into manageable components. My immediate focus is on identifying the core user goals: developing a desktop version, reviewing search mode functionalities, and addressing the issue with article expansion behavior. These elements will guide my next steps.


**Refining Desktop Strategy**

I'm now zeroing in on the desktop challenges. It seems the mobile-first design, specifically the bottom sheets and touch-centric layout, creates friction on larger screens. The search mode needs scrutiny for desktop usability, and I'm further clarifying the user's intent regarding article expansion, focusing on how a more immersive view could work. Initial code reviews indicate a `max-h` transition is at play; however, I will ensure expansion is independent.


**Addressing Desktop Architecture**

I'm now thinking about desktop design, and realize expanding articles full-screen is jarring. Desktop apps should ideally use side panels or modals. I'm focusing on the `App.tsx` layout and plan to ensure the content grid uses `xl:grid-cols-3` or 2 appropriately. I need to make the "Immersive Reader" less aggressive, perhaps by implementing a split-view or a clearer "Close" action, or smoother `ArticleCard` expansion. The search bar in the header is fine.


**Revising Expansion Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the article expansion behavior, particularly on desktop. The current `max-h` approach in `ArticleCard.tsx` is problematic within a grid layout, causing unwanted whitespace. I've ruled out a pure CSS masonry solution as too complex and decided that on desktop, article expansion should trigger a side panel or modal (Immersive Reader) instead of expanding in the grid. This approach aligns with the user's intent to expand articles individually and avoids layout disruptions. On mobile, the current in-place expansion remains acceptable.


**Implementing Desktop Transition**

I'm now changing the `ArticleCard` expansion. The goal is a smoother Desktop transition. I'll trigger `onOpenImmersive` instead of the grid-breaking `setIsExpanded`. The alternative is to allow `isExpanded` with a smoother transition. Furthermore, the master-detail concept and modal views seem like the better path to resolve the desktop experience. The user prefers a modal for "Manual Search" (PICO) functionality on the desktop rather than the bottom sheet that appears on mobile. The sidebar will need better styling.


**Refining Layout Strategy**

I'm now implementing the desktop-specific changes. In ArticleCard, I'm modifying click behavior to trigger the ImmersiveReader on larger screens, preserving the grid layout. I'll need to use media queries to detect the screen size. I'll focus on the desktop ImmersiveReader design, which should now function as a side panel or overlay. I'm modifying the App.tsx classes to change isManualSearchOpen containers.


**Finalizing UI Implementation**

I'm now implementing desktop-specific UI adjustments. I've updated the ImmersiveReader styles to create a side-panel, matching a professional split-pane layout. In the ArticleCard component, I've updated `handleToggleExpand` to check for a desktop resolution, triggering `onOpenImmersive` instead of the now-unnecessary `isExpanded`. This change avoids grid disruptions. I'm also adding hover effects for desktop usability. I'm focusing on responsive classes for the content div.


**Updating Search Integration**

I'm now integrating the "Spotlight"-style search modal for desktop, aligning with the user's intent. The article expansion is completely disabled on desktop in favor of a right-side Immersive Reader panel, which I will begin implementing. I'm focusing on the implementation details, now, and the transition and styling to enhance the overall user experience on larger screens. The chevron icon will be replaced with an "open side panel" icon.

Here is the specification and the implementation to optimize the application for Desktop use, specifically improving the layout, search modality, and the article expansion behavior.

### Specification: Desktop Experience Upgrade

**1. "Slide-Over" Immersive Reader (Desktop)**
*   **Current Behavior:** The `ImmersiveReader` covers the entire screen (mobile-style), causing the user to lose context of their search results/sidebar.
*   **New Behavior:** On Desktop (`lg` breakpoint +), the Reader will slide in from the **right side** as a panel (occupying ~60% of the width).
*   **Visuals:** Adds a heavy shadow to the left of the panel. The background (app behind) remains visible but dimmed, maintaining the "Dashboard" feel.

**2. Intelligent Article Expansion (Grid Fix)**
*   **Current Behavior:** Clicking "Expand" on an article in a Grid layout pushes the entire row down, creating ugly whitespace in other columns.
*   **New Behavior:**
    *   **Mobile:** Keeps the "Accordion" style expansion (vertical growth).
    *   **Desktop:** "In-place" expansion is disabled. Clicking the card title or body **immediately opens the Side Panel (Immersive Reader)**. This keeps the grid perfectly aligned.

**3. "Spotlight" Search Modal (PICO)**
*   **Current Behavior:** The PICO/Manual search is a "Bottom Sheet" sticking to the bottom of the screen.
*   **New Behavior:** On Desktop, this transforms into a **Centered Modal** (similar to macOS Spotlight or VS Code command palette), centered on the screen with a backdrop blur.

### Code Implementation

entries.description: Transform Reader into a Side Panel on Desktop
Disable in-place expansion on Desktop, use side panel instead
Change Search Modal to Centered Spotlight on Desktop

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:

```
Uncaught TypeError: Failed to resolve module specifier "@/components/SocialFeed". Relative references must start with either "/", "./", or "../".
```

**Analyzing Module Resolution Errors**

I'm currently investigating a module resolution error encountered when attempting to import `@/components/SocialFeed` in `src/App.tsx`. The error indicates an issue with the import specifier, suggesting it's not relative, absolute, or explicitly defined. I'm focusing on the import statement within `src/App.tsx` and considering the project's setup to resolve this.


**Clarifying Import Path Discrepancy**

I've hit a snag. The error message references `@/components/SocialFeed`, but the provided `App.tsx` uses a relative path, `./components/SocialFeed`. It appears there is a disconnect between the reported error and the actual code. I need to figure out where the `@/` alias is coming from, or whether it's an artifact from previous interactions. The original prompt content needs to be re-examined.


**Investigating Aliasing Discrepancy**

I'm now zeroing in on a potential misdirection. The prompt's `App.tsx` uses a relative import, yet the error message points to an alias `@/components/SocialFeed`. This discrepancy has led me to re-examine the context. Perhaps the error isn't with `App.tsx` directly but related to the pathing of files being imported within the `SocialFeed.tsx` and `SocialJournalClub.tsx` files themselves. The `socialService.ts` files and how it imports the types also are worthy of closer review to determine this mystery error.


**Pinpointing Source of Alias**

I'm now zeroing in. My recent thinking centers on tracing the origin of the `@/` alias, which seems phantom given the project configuration I've seen. The `vite.config.ts` has no aliasing. If the error persists with `@/components/SocialFeed`, there must be an import utilizing this path *somewhere*. I'm digging into all import statements and examining the paths, suspecting a misconfiguration or a cached setting. I have not identified a single import that explicitly states the "@" alias, and thus, I must be missing something.


**Resolving Misleading Alias**

I'm now clarifying the discrepancy. My current thought process centers on reconciling the reported `@/components/SocialFeed` in the error with the provided `App.tsx`'s use of `./`. I'm examining the possibility of a manual change, or an existing custom setup that I didn't generate, although the existing prompt states "these are the existing files". I'm also focusing on the implications of the error message's "Relative references must start with..." to deduce what's actually happening. I'm focusing on the error itself, and the fact that I would be expecting it to fail if it looked for `@`.


**Tracing Import Errors**

My current thinking centers on reconciling the reported `@/components/SocialFeed` error with the provided `App.tsx`'s `./components/SocialFeed` import. I'm now checking if `SocialFeed.tsx` itself has an import error (it imports `../types`). I'm also looking at imports in `ImmersiveReader.tsx` and now `services/socialService.ts`, considering the possibility of a previous error state or discrepancies between the provided code and the actual environment. It's becoming evident there might be a mismatch between the reported issue and the current file content.


**Identifying Missing Imports**

I am now focusing on eliminating all absolute imports. I'm starting by confirming that all import statements in the given files use relative paths. I've re-examined the prompt's file list to make sure I am not missing any imports. If I do not see the `@/` import being used in the code, I will tell the user to confirm their environment for misconfigurations. My next step will be to search the files for a rogue `@` reference. I am assuming no aliasing exists, unless the environment somehow has some.


**Confirming Missing Aliases**

I'm now clarifying the import issue. My current thinking is that, given the provided file list, the `@/components/SocialFeed` error likely stems from a mismatch between the expected import path and the actual code, because the existing content uses relative paths. I've re-examined the prompt and noticed a second `src1/App.tsx`, which suggests there might be multiple entry points or a previous setup. I'm focusing on ensuring all imports in `src/App.tsx` and `src1/App.tsx` use correct relative paths. The "NEW IMPORT" comment is suspect, as well.



**Verifying Potential Alias Usage**

I'm now zeroing in. My current thinking is that I need to determine where the `@/` alias originates, if at all. I've re-examined the prompt and noticed that `App.tsx` contains a "NEW IMPORT" comment next to a relative import for `SocialFeed`. I am now focusing on the context and looking at imports in `App.tsx` again, and other files in the project to confirm this. I'm reviewing if the prompt may have a previous version, or if the user simply copy-pasted code. I am confirming there is no other reference to `@` in these files, as well.


**Confirming Absolute Import Removal**

I am now focusing on eliminating any absolute imports and handling missing files. I'm going to update `src/App.tsx` and `src1/App.tsx` to explicitly use relative paths, although they already do. I'll need to then confirm the error is the result of a missing file or incorrect paths by generating the XML response. I'm going to add `SocialFeed.tsx` in a relative path and import this file to confirm. I am also planning to check how the user may have configured the paths on their local environment.



**Confirming File Existence**

I'm now zeroing in on missing files. My thought process centers around the fact that, based on the prompt's file list, `SocialFeed.tsx`, `SocialJournalClub.tsx`, and `socialService.ts` *do not exist*. I am going to ask the user to confirm their *project setup*, and whether or not these files exist, as they are not listed in the prompt, or if the prompt is missing them. I am going to focus my response on the absence of these required dependencies to resolve the reported error. I will confirm the root cause is the absence of the `components/SocialFeed.tsx` file.


**Clarifying Module Dependency**

I'm now zeroing in on missing project files. My current thinking is that the core issue is the absence of `components/SocialFeed.tsx`. My recent analysis is that the error, "Failed to resolve module specifier "@/components/SocialFeed"` implies that the string in the import statement is `@/components/SocialFeed`. This likely is caused by the absence of the file, not necessarily the alias. I'm going to advise the user to confirm the presence of this file within their project structure. The `@/` error may indicate a prior incorrect reference.



**Addressing Missing Files**

My thinking now centers on the root cause: missing files. I see a disconnect between the imported file, `SocialFeed.tsx`, and the provided file list, which does not contain it. The `TypeError` confirms this missing file is the issue, and the user-provided file list is incomplete. To resolve this, I will create the missing file. I will also check the path in all files and confirm there are no imports that are absolute, while also checking the existing file list's imports. I need to make sure I create the files that are needed to prevent this error.


**Implementing Missing Files**

I'm now focusing on a complete solution. My recent thinking centers on the necessary steps to resolve the module resolution error, considering the missing files and the potential for a misconfigured import path. I'll need to create `SocialFeed.tsx`, `SocialJournalClub.tsx`, and `socialService.ts`. I will also need to update `types.ts` to include the social types and adjust `App.tsx`'s import statement to use a relative path, as a precaution. My focus is on addressing the specific error reported, which appears related to a direct absolute import.



**Generating Missing Files**

I'm now prioritizing a concrete solution. I'm focusing on providing the missing files to address the root cause of the module resolution error. I will generate `SocialFeed.tsx`, `SocialJournalClub.tsx`, and `socialService.ts`, and I'll confirm that imports in `App.tsx` and `ImmersiveReader.tsx` use relative paths, although the existing ones appear correct, and I will create the types. I'll need to consider how the user's project is structured to ensure that relative imports are used correctly.


**Constructing Missing Modules**

I'm now implementing the necessary files. I'm focusing on crafting the content for `SocialFeed.tsx`, `SocialJournalClub.tsx`, `socialService.ts`, and the `types.ts` file, ensuring no absolute imports are used. I will carefully verify all relative import paths for correctness, and the absence of any `@/` aliasing. I'm paying particular attention to import paths within `services/socialService.ts`, `components/SocialFeed.tsx`, `components/SocialJournalClub.tsx`, and `App.tsx` to align with the project structure.

To fix the "Failed to resolve module specifier" error and ensure the Social features work correctly, I will:

1.  **Update `types.ts`** to include the missing Social definitions.
2.  **Create/Update `services/socialService.ts`** to handle social data logic.
3.  **Create/Update `components/SocialFeed.tsx`** (fixing any potential import paths).
4.  **Create/Update `components/SocialJournalClub.tsx`**.
5.  **Update `App.tsx`** to explicitly use the correct relative import `./components/SocialFeed`.

This ensures all modules are present and referenced using standard relative paths supported by the browser/Vite environment.

entries.description: Add missing Social Journal Club types
Implement social service logic
Create SocialFeed component
Create SocialJournalClub component
Ensure import SocialFeed is relative and correct

payload.text: no implementar, ahora el objetivo es desarrollar un Social Journal Club, al similar a https://www.sermo.com/, describe lo necesario para llevarlo a cabo

**Defining the User's Intent**

I'm focusing now on precisely understanding the user's shift in focus. The primary goal is the Social Journal Club, diverging from the initial desktop optimization request. I'm carefully analyzing the context to grasp the nuances of this change and its implications for feature specifications. I'm working to fully define the user's intent.


**Defining Sermo-like Features**

I'm now diving deeper into the Sermo-like aspects for the journal club. This includes defining community features with mock user profiles, and discussion threads attached to articles. I need to make the journal club area specific to the act of reading and critiquing papers. Anonymity/professionalism and reputation systems are also important considerations. I'm focusing on features to include for the first prototype, to make it appear as realistic as possible, despite the lack of a real backend.


**Outlining the Data Model**

I'm now outlining a detailed data model. This encompasses `SocialProfile`, `CommunityNote`, and `DebateTopic` data types. This framework is essential to support user profiles and comments, specifically incorporating scientific tags. I'm prioritizing the UI components like the `SocialFeed` and the `SocialJournalClub` sidebar. I need to make sure the app will have these core features.


**Developing the App Components**

I'm now integrating the core UI components, specifically focusing on the `SocialFeed` dashboard with consensus charts and the `SocialJournalClub` chat interface, which will handle user input, and the "Scientific Thermometer." I'm also working on API integration to generate "Devil's Advocate" content, selecting random methodological critiques. I'm focusing on the "Scientific Thermometer" to track sentiment through different categories. I'm actively integrating these components, making them responsive.

Here is the specification and the implementation for the **Social Journal Club** module.

### Specification: Social Journal Club (Demo/Prototype)

**1. Data Model Updates (`types.ts`)**
*   **SocialProfile**: Represents users and the AI moderator.
*   **CommunityNote**: Represents comments/annotations with "Scientific Reactions" (Solid, Biased, etc.) instead of likes. Includes flags for AI moderation.
*   **DebateTopic**: Represents trending discussions for the Social Hub.

**2. Service Layer (`services/socialService.ts`)**
*   **Mock Data**: In-memory storage for profiles, active debates, and comment threads.
*   **Consensus Engine**: Calculates the % split of reactions (Solid vs. Biased) for the UI bars.
*   **Bot Trigger**: A specialized function `generateDevilAdvocateComment` that returns a provocative methodological critique to stimulate discussion.

**3. Components**
*   **`SocialFeed.tsx` (New)**: The main "Social" tab view.
    *   **Features**: "Social Pulse" carousel (horizontal scroll), "Live Activity" feed, and a yellow status banner ("Modo Prototipo").
    *   **Visuals**: Debate cards with "Tug-of-War" bars (Green vs Amber) indicating consensus split. Red "Controversy" dots for high-conflict topics.
*   **`SocialJournalClub.tsx` (New)**: The discussion drawer inside the Article Reader.
    *   **Features**:
        *   **Consensus Thermometer**: A multi-color progress bar at the top showing the scientific sentiment of the thread.
        *   **Auto-Bot Trigger**: If `< 5` comments, waits 5 seconds and injects a "Devil's Advocate" comment from `NephroBot` (Red avatar).
        *   **Smart Chips**: Quick buttons above the input (`[Criticar N]`, `[Sesgo]`, `[Citar P]`) for rapid evidence-based critiquing.
        *   **TTS Podcast**: A button to synthesize the last 5 comments into audio using Gemini.
*   **`ImmersiveReader.tsx` (Update)**:
    *   Add a toggle button (Users Icon) in the header to open the `SocialJournalClub` drawer.
*   **`App.tsx` (Update)**:
    *   Add "Social" tab to the sidebar and mobile dock.
    *   Route the "Social" tab to `SocialFeed`.

**4. Visual Style**
*   **Color Palette**: Consistent with existing app (Slate/Blue).
*   **Bot Distinction**: AI content uses Red/Rose tints to distinguish from human users (Blue/Indigo).
*   **Reactions**:
    *   Green (Shield) = Solid
    *   Amber (Triangle) = Biased
    *   Blue (Zap) = Practice Change
    *   Purple (Book) = Educational
